title: CP-101 Knowledge Hub
summary: Single entry point for the CP101 biostatistics and studyoperations knowledge base. Start here to navigate the study's design, analysis, data, and delivery artifacts.

title: Protocol
summary: Authoritative description of the CP101 firstinhuman SAD/MAD design in healthy participants; the source document all downstream specs must trace to.

title: Schedule of Assessments
summary: Defines what is assessed at each CP101 visit and timepoint, by cohort and dosing day, for both the SAD and MAD phases.

title: eCRF Specification
summary: Fieldlevel specification for CP101 electronic data capture: forms, variables, controlled terminology, and editcheck intent.

title: PK Sampling Plan
summary: Defines the PK bloodsampling strategy for CP101 across the SAD and MAD cohorts, including nominal timepoints, window tolerances, and handling of deviations.

title: Statistical Analysis Plan
summary: The master statistical methods document for CP101, governing analysis populations, endpoints, and the planned summaries for the SAD/MAD firstinhuman design.

title: TLF Shells
summary: Mock tables, listings, and figures for CP101 — the emptybutfullyformatted output templates that define titles, structure, footnotes, and population labels with no data po…

title: ADaM Specifications
summary: Analysis dataset specifications for CP101 — the ADaMlevel metadata defining derived variables, parameters, and traceability from collected data to analysisready structure…

title: Data Management Plan
summary: Governs how CP101 clinical data are captured, cleaned, transferred, and locked across the study lifecycle.

title: EDC Build
summary: Tracks configuration of the CP101 EDC system from approved specs through golive and migration control.

title: Edit Checks
summary: Specifies the automated and manual datavalidation rules applied in the CP101 EDC to flag discrepancies at or near entry.

title: Data Transfers
summary: Governs how external data reaches CP101's clinical database, including transfer formats, frequency, file naming, and reconciliation.

title: Lab Data Transfers
summary: Governs the operational handling of central laboratory data transfers into CP101 (safety chemistry/hematology, urinalysis, and PK bioanalytical results).

title: Database Lock
summary: Defines the readiness criteria and sequencing for CP101 database lock(s), including any SAD/MAD interim locks ahead of the final lock.

title: Monitoring Plan
summary: Defines how CP101 safety and conduct are monitored across the SAD and MAD dosing periods.

title: Risk Signals
summary: Catalogs the predefined safety and conduct signals that trigger escalation in CP101.

title: DSMB Operations
summary: Describes how the Data Safety Monitoring Board is convened and serviced for CP101.

title: Interim Analysis (ops)
summary: Plans the operational mechanics of any interim safety look in CP101.

title: Randomization & Blinding
summary: Defines how participants are assigned to active or placebo and how the blind is maintained across the CP101 SAD/MAD program.

title: Drug Supply & Accountability
summary: Tracks investigational product receipt, storage, dispensing, reconciliation, and return for CP101.

title: CSR Plan
summary: Defines how CP101 moves from final TLF outputs to a completed Clinical Study Report.

title: Milestones
summary: Single tracked timeline of CP101 statistical and reporting milestones.

title: Lessons Learned
summary: Running log of what worked, what slipped, and what to change for the next CP101 cohort and future protocols.

title: Roles & Responsibilities
summary: Defines who owns each deliverable, decision, and signoff across the CP101 biostatistics workstream.

title: Naming Conventions
summary: Sets the file, dataset, variable, and output naming standards every CP101 deliverable must follow.

title: Glossary
summary: Single source of truth for CP101 abbreviations, role labels, and studyspecific terms.

title: Vendors
summary: Lists the external service providers supporting CP101 and the biostatisticsrelevant interfaces with each.
